data.title: Binance will adjust the portfolio margin collateral ratio
data.description: According to the official announcement, Binance will adjust the collateral ratio of DENT, ENJ, NOT, DASH, CHZ, AXS, ENS, SAND, THETA, QNT and other assets under the portfolio margin model at 06:00 (UTC) on April 18, 2025. The adjustment will be completed in about 1 hour. (This newsletter is generated by AI)

data.title: After Winklevoss Capital transferred 3,750 ETH to Galaxy Digital, the latter transferred 12,500 ETH to Binance
data.description: The Data Nerd's monitoring shows that 14 hours ago, Winklevoss Capital transferred about 3,750 ETH (about 5.84 million US dollars) to Galaxy Digital; 5 hours later, Galaxy Digital recharged 12,500 ETH (about 19.74 million US dollars) to Binance.

data.title: Trader Eugene: I have already shorted ETH, SOL, and XRP when BTC reached 83,500, and I don’t think the market is sustainable
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Well-known trader Eugene said in the Telegram channel: "When Bitcoin hit $83,500 at night, I established a small number of ETH, SOL and XRP short positions. Unless the Federal Reserve intervenes explicitly or the Chinese tariff issue is properly eased, I don't think the market will have much sustainability."

data.title: Spanish police bust $21 million cryptocurrency scam
data.description: Spanish police have arrested six people in a two-year operation, dismantling a cryptocurrency investment fraud ring that used artificial intelligence to defraud 208 people of more than 19 million euros ($21.5 million). Authorities said the suspects used realistic celebrity videos created with AI technology to promise huge returns through crypto investments. The suspects have been charged with fraud, money laundering and false documents, and the investigation is ongoing. (Decrypt)

data.title: Movement's Total Value Locked Surpasses $100 Million
data.description: According to Foresight News, data from DefiLlama indicates that the total value locked (TVL) in Movement has exceeded $100 million. The current TVL is reported to be $124.41 million.

data.title: US crypto industry needs band-aid now, 'long-term solution' later — Uyeda
data.description:
A fast-tracked temporary crypto regulatory framework could bolster innovation within the US crypto industry while permanent regulations are still in the works, says acting US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) chair Mark Uyeda. “A time-limited, conditional exemptive relief framework for registrants and non-registrants could allow for greater innovation with blockchain technology within the United States in the near term,” Uyeda said at the SEC’s April 11 Crypto Task Force roundtable titled “Between a Block and a Hard Place: Tailoring Regulation for Crypto Trading. ”Relief measures may address immediate challengesUyeda said this might be the short-term answer as the SEC works toward a “long-term solution,” speaking at the roundtable event with agency members and crypto industry executives, including Uniswap Labs’ Katherine Minarik, Cumberland DRW’s Chelsea Pizzola, and Coinbase’s Gregory Tusar. He flagged state-by-state regulation of crypto trading as a concern, warning it could lead to a “patchwork of state licensing regimes
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/mark-uyeda-crypto-temporary-framework-sec-roundtable-crypto-task-force?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: McDonald's wrote to the US SEC: No intention to discuss Bitcoin reserve proposals at the May shareholders meeting
data.description: In response to the proposal to store Bitcoin previously requested by McDonald's shareholders, the National Center for Public Policy, McDonald's legal representatives wrote to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) stating that the company does not want to discuss the proposal to purchase Bitcoin at the upcoming shareholders' meeting in May. The SEC agreed that McDonald's has the right to avoid discussing the proposal. (The Block)

data.title: SEC Considers Temporary Crypto Regulation Framework
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission's (SEC) Acting Chairman Mark Uyeda suggested the possibility of establishing a temporary regulatory framework for cryptocurrencies. Speaking at an event at the SEC's Washington headquarters, Uyeda emphasized the need for a short-term approach that would allow businesses to continue innovating while the agency works on a more permanent regulatory solution for digital assets.

Uyeda proposed exploring whether a more effective regulatory method could exist under a less stringent federal framework. During a recent cryptocurrency industry roundtable, he mentioned that a time-limited, conditional exemption framework for both registered and non-registered entities might encourage greater blockchain innovation within the United States in the short term, as the commission develops long-term strategies to address these issues.
